description: Global custodial-operator registry (ADR-079). Maps a well-known custodial hot-wallet operator address to a human-labeled EXTERNAL_CUSTODY counterparty (ADR-072 model). Distinct from ADR-072's per-session, user-designated externalCustodyDestinations: this is a maintained global default for shared, well-known operator pools a user cannot be expected to configure (e.g. Telegram Wallet). Keyed by TON address in ANY canonical form (raw workchain 0:hex or friendly EQ/UQ) — TonAddressCanonicalizer expands each configured key to its equivalent forms. TON addresses are case-sensitive and never lowercased. Detection is registry-deterministic; tonapi account name/interfaces and the wallet_highload_v3r1 interface are OFFLINE DISCOVERY AIDS ONLY used to seed/extend this file — never queried on the per-row pipeline path. The highload interface alone is NEVER sufficient for a custodial label: an operator not listed here stays UNKNOWN_EOA (never guessed as Telegram). Existing deterministic classifiers keep priority (Bybit highload -> AccountingUniverseService EXCHANGE_ACCOUNT; TON DEX routers -> TonProtocolRegistry family DEX).
